text.title: Message Broker Implementations Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real mq-broker-implementations deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Producer calls basic_publish but never registers a confirm callback; broker restart loses in-flight messages silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copy-pasted a basic example missing publisher_confirms_select() or its equivalent.
antipattern.fix: Enable publisher confirms on the channel; treat unconfirmed messages as a fail and resend.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer acks the message before committing the DB write; DB fail then drops the side-effect with no retry.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated ack as 'received' rather than 'processed'.
antipattern.fix: Always ack AFTER the side-effect is committed. On commit failure, nack with requeue.

antipattern.symptom: Poison message blocks the queue or is dropped on max-retries with no audit trail.
antipattern.fix: DLQ + 5-min alert wired before the queue goes live; non-negotiable.
